heading: Opioid use disorder treatment demonstration program
label: 1395cc–6 Opioid use disorder treatment demonstration program

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall implement a 4-year demonstration program under this subchapter (in this section referred to as the “Program”) to increase access of applicable beneficiaries to opioid use disorder treatment services, improve physical and mental health outcomes for such beneficiaries, and to the extent possible, reduce expenditures under this subchapter. Under the Program, the Secretary shall make payments under subsection (e) to participants (as defined in subsection (c)(1)(A)) for furnishing opioid use disorder treatment services delivered through opioid use disorder care teams, or arranging for such services to be furnished, to applicable beneficiaries participating in the Program.January 1, 2021

children.label: A
children.text: means, with respect to an applicable beneficiary, services that are furnished for the treatment of opioid use disorders and that utilize drugs approved under for the treatment of opioid use disorders in an outpatient setting; andsection 355 of title 21

children.label: i
children.text: medication-assisted treatment;

children.label: ii
children.text: treatment planning;

children.label: iii
children.text: psychiatric, psychological, or counseling services (or any combination of such services), as appropriate;

children.label: iv
children.text: social support services, as appropriate; and

children.label: v
children.text: care management and care coordination services, including coordination with other providers of services and suppliers not on an opioid use disorder care team.

children.label: A
children.text: Reduces hospitalizations and emergency department visits.

children.label: B
children.text: Increases use of medication-assisted treatment for opioid use disorders.

children.label: C
children.text: Improves health outcomes of individuals with opioid use disorders, including by reducing the incidence of infectious diseases (such as hepatitis C and HIV).

children.label: D
children.text: Does not increase the total spending on items and services under this subchapter.

children.label: E
children.text: Reduces deaths from opioid overdose.

children.label: F
children.text: Reduces the utilization of inpatient residential treatment.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 2 Consultation
children.text: In designing the Program, including the criteria under subsection (e)(2)(A), the Secretary shall, not later than 3 months after , consult with specialists in the field of addiction, clinicians in the primary care community, and beneficiary groups.October 24, 2018

children.heading: Participants; opioid use disorder care teams
children.label: c Participants; opioid use disorder care teams
I
children.text: a physician (as defined in );section 1395x(r)(1) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: a group practice comprised of at least one physician described in subclause (I);

children.label: III
children.text: a hospital outpatient department;

children.label: IV
children.text: a federally qualified health center (as defined in );section 1395x(aa)(4) of this title

children.label: V
children.text: a rural health clinic (as defined in );section 1395x(aa)(2) of this title

children.label: VI
children.text: a community mental health center (as defined in );section 1395x(ff)(3)(B) of this title

children.label: VII
children.text: a clinic certified as a certified community behavioral health clinic pursuant to section 223 of the Protecting Access to Medicare Act of 2014; or

children.label: VIII
children.text: any other individual or entity specified by the Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: that applied for and was selected to participate in the Program pursuant to an application and selection process established by the Secretary; and

children.label: iii
children.text: that establishes an opioid use disorder care team (as defined in paragraph (2)) through employing or contracting with health care practitioners described in paragraph (2)(A), and uses such team to furnish or arrange for opioid use disorder treatment services in the outpatient setting under the Program.

children.heading: Preference
children.label: B Preference
children.text: In selecting participants for the Program, the Secretary shall give preference to individuals and entities that are located in areas with a prevalence of opioid use disorders that is higher than the national average prevalence.

children.label: I
children.text: at least one physician (as defined in ) furnishing primary care services or addiction treatment services to an applicable beneficiary; andsection 1395x(r)(1) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: at least one eligible practitioner (as defined in paragraph (3)), who may be a physician who meets the criterion in subclause (I); and

children.label: ii
children.text: may include other practitioners licensed under State law to furnish psychiatric, psychological, counseling, and social services to applicable beneficiaries.

children.heading: Requirements for receipt of payment under program
children.label: B Requirements for receipt of payment under program
i
children.text: furnish opioid use disorder treatment services through opioid use disorder care teams to applicable beneficiaries who agree to receive the services;

children.label: ii
children.text: meet minimum criteria, as established by the Secretary; and

children.label: I
children.text: monitor and evaluate the Program;

children.label: II
children.text: determine if minimum criteria are met under clause (ii); and

children.label: III
children.text: determine the incentive payment under subsection (e).

children.label: A
children.text: is enrolled under ; andsection 1395cc(j)(1) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: is authorized to prescribe or dispense narcotic drugs to individuals for maintenance treatment or detoxification treatment.

children.label: A
children.text: is entitled to, or enrolled for, benefits under part A and enrolled for benefits under part B;

children.label: B
children.text: is not enrolled in a Medicare Advantage plan under part C;

children.label: C
children.text: has a current diagnosis for an opioid use disorder; and

children.label: D
children.text: meets such other criteria as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Voluntary beneficiary participation; limitation on number of beneficiaries
children.label: 2 Voluntary beneficiary participation; limitation on number of beneficiaries
children.text: An applicable beneficiary may participate in the Program on a voluntary basis and may terminate participation in the Program at any time. Not more than 20,000 applicable beneficiaries may participate in the Program at any time.

children.heading: Services
children.label: 3 Services
children.text: In order to participate in the Program, an applicable beneficiary shall agree to receive opioid use disorder treatment services from a participant. Participation under the Program shall not affect coverage of or payment for any other item or service under this subchapter for the applicable beneficiary.

children.heading: Beneficiary access to services
children.label: 4 Beneficiary access to services
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as encouraging providers to limit applicable beneficiary access to services covered under this subchapter, and applicable beneficiaries shall not be required to relinquish access to any benefit under this subchapter as a condition of receiving services from a participant in the Program.

children.heading: Per applicable beneficiary per month care management fee
children.label: 1 Per applicable beneficiary per month care management fee

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a schedule of per applicable beneficiary per month care management fees. Such a per applicable beneficiary per month care management fee shall be paid to a participant in addition to any other amount otherwise payable under this subchapter to the health care practitioners in the participant’s opioid use disorder care team or, if applicable, to the participant. A participant may use such per applicable beneficiary per month care management fee to deliver additional services to applicable beneficiaries, including services not otherwise eligible for payment under this subchapter.

children.label: i
children.text: consider payments otherwise payable under this subchapter for opioid use disorder treatment services and the needs of applicable beneficiaries;

children.label: ii
children.text: pay a higher per applicable beneficiary per month care management fee for an applicable beneficiary who receives more intensive treatment services from a participant and for whom those services are appropriate based on clinical guidelines for opioid use disorder care;

children.label: iii
children.text: pay a higher per applicable beneficiary per month care management fee for the month in which the applicable beneficiary begins treatment with a participant than in subsequent months, to reflect the greater time and costs required for the planning and initiation of treatment, as compared to maintenance of treatment; and

children.label: iv
children.text: take into account whether a participant’s opioid use disorder care team refers applicable beneficiaries to other suppliers or providers for any opioid use disorder treatment services.

children.heading: No duplicate payment
children.label: C No duplicate payment
children.text: The Secretary shall make payments under this paragraph to only one participant for services furnished to an applicable beneficiary during a calendar month.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Under the Program, the Secretary shall establish a performance-based incentive payment, which shall be paid (using a methodology established and at a time determined appropriate by the Secretary) to participants based on the performance of participants with respect to criteria, as determined appropriate by the Secretary, in accordance with subparagraph (B).

children.label: I
children.text: Patient engagement and retention in treatment.

children.label: II
children.text: Evidence-based medication-assisted treatment.

children.label: III
children.text: Other criteria established by the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: consult with stakeholders, including clinicians in the primary care community and in the field of addiction medicine; and

children.label: II
children.text: consider existing clinical guidelines for the treatment of opioid use disorders.

children.heading: No duplicate payment
children.label: C No duplicate payment
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that no duplicate payments under this paragraph are made with respect to an applicable beneficiary.

children.heading: Multipayer strategy
children.label: f Multipayer strategy
children.text: In carrying out the Program, the Secretary shall encourage other payers to provide similar payments and to use similar criteria as applied under the Program under subsection (e)(2)(C). The Secretary may enter into a memorandum of understanding with other payers to align the methodology for payment provided by such a payer related to opioid use disorder treatment services with such methodology for payment under the Program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct an intermediate and final evaluation of the program. Each such evaluation shall determine the extent to which each of the purposes described in subsection (b) have been accomplished under the Program.

children.label: A
children.text: a report with respect to the intermediate evaluation under paragraph (1) not later than 3 years after the date of the implementation of the Program; and

children.label: B
children.text: a report with respect to the final evaluation under paragraph (1) not later than 6 years after such date.

children.heading: Administrative funding
children.label: 1 Administrative funding
children.text: For the purposes of implementing, administering, and carrying out the Program (other than for purposes described in paragraph (2)), $5,000,000 shall be available from the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund under .section 1395t of this title

children.heading: Care management fees and incentives
children.label: 2 Care management fees and incentives
children.text: For the purposes of making payments under subsection (e), $10,000,000 shall be available from the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund under for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2024.section 1395t of this title

children.heading: Availability
children.label: 3 Availability
children.text: Amounts transferred under this subsection for a fiscal year shall be available until expended.

children.heading: Waivers
children.label: i Waivers
children.text: The Secretary may waive any provision of this subchapter as may be necessary to carry out the Program under this section.
